_source: International Astronomical Union (IAU) Minor Planet Center Observatory Codes and official observatory websites - Real coordinates verified against Google Earth
_description: Major astronomical observatories worldwide with precise coordinates, altitude, and IAU observatory codes

observatories.name: Mauna Kea Observatories
observatories.notes: Premier astronomical site; above 40% of atmosphere. Exceptional seeing conditions.

observatories.name: Paranal Observatory
observatories.notes: Home of ESO Very Large Telescope. One of driest and darkest sites on Earth.

observatories.name: La Silla Observatory
observatories.notes: ESO's first observatory in Chile. Operational since 1969.

observatories.name: Cerro Tololo Inter-American Observatory
observatories.notes: NOIRLab facility. Home of the Dark Energy Survey with DECam.

observatories.name: Roque de los Muchachos Observatory
observatories.notes: Northern hemisphere premier site. Gran Telescopio Canarias is world's largest single-aperture optical telescope.

observatories.name: Mount Wilson Observatory
observatories.notes: Historic observatory where Hubble discovered the expanding universe in 1929.

observatories.name: Palomar Observatory
observatories.notes: Home of the Hale Telescope, which was the world's largest from 1949-1993.

observatories.name: Arecibo Observatory
observatories.notes: Former world's largest single-dish radio telescope. Collapsed December 1, 2020.

observatories.name: Siding Spring Observatory
observatories.notes: Australia's premier optical and infrared observatory.

observatories.name: South African Astronomical Observatory
observatories.notes: Home of Southern African Large Telescope, largest single optical telescope in Southern Hemisphere.

observatories.name: Royal Greenwich Observatory
observatories.notes: Home of the Prime Meridian. Historical site where GMT was established. Now a museum.

observatories.name: Atacama Large Millimeter Array (ALMA)
observatories.notes: World's largest astronomical project. 66 high-precision radio antennas on the Chajnantor plateau.
